summary: Traced all 34 business requirements (R1–R34) from the Define-phase requirements artifact against the diff (new Phases::Pause / RerunMenuStep / RestartDefine services, Phases::Convergence query, ManagerTick pause/restart handling, PhasesController pause/rerun_step/restart actions + routes, Approve/AnswerQuestions status widening, the paused status enum + migration columns, and the _define_panel menu UI). Every requirement is satisfied with concrete evidence. Pausing (R1–R6): a Pause control shows while running; Pause flags a request and ManagerTick#settle_pause holds only after the in-flight step finishes (R2/R3), a paused phase makes ManagerTick early-return so the auto loop cannot advance (R5/R27), and paused state is shown with a status badge + explicit 'Define is paused' label, not color alone (R4). The five-item menu (R7) is present: Explore/Clarifying Questions re-run the step that declares discovery_notes/open_questions by artifact identity and surface fresh output inline (R8–R11), with an in-progress indicator replacing the menu while any re-run runs, matching the restart treatment (R12); Ask Human reuses AnswerQuestions to show open questions and fold answers/notes in as human-tagged feedback (R13/R14); Repeat from the Beginning restarts from the first worker step, shows a 'restart in progress' state, overwrites with fresh output, carries human feedback forward via restart_feedback, and lands back on the paused menu on convergence (R15–R19); Done maps to Approve, gated live by Phases::Convergence.phase_settled? so it only ends Define when settled and otherwise reports :not_settled (R20/R21). Step-through control holds the phase paused after each action (R22–R24). Failure handling keeps the phase paused (abort_restart for restart, no auto-retry while paused) and surfaces a labeled 'Re-run failed' badge + message (R25/R26). Safeguards: any_step_active?/busy guards on every menu service plus the unique (step_id,iteration,attempt) index prevent overlap and menu/auto conflicts (R29/R30), and manual re-runs create runs directly without passing through route_to_target's max_iterations check, so a human clicking a menu action is never itself capped (R28). Existing gate approval, send-back rework, and clarifying-question answering are left intact, only additively widened (R31–R34). One nuance on R28: manual re-runs share the per-step iteration counter that the automatic cap reads, so they can influence the escalation point — but only ever during a subsequent Repeat-from-the-Beginning restart (the auto loop and manual re-runs never run in the same phase state otherwise), and that restart-vs-cap fall-through to awaiting_human is an explicitly documented, accepted design decision; the core intent of R28 (manual actions are never refused or capped) holds. No blocking, major, or minor conformance deviations.
